commands.review_summary:
The PR adds one same-profile overloaded retry with backoff for no-fallback embedded-agent runs and covers prompt-side, assistant-side, and retry-exhaustion behavior.

PR surface: Source +85, Tests +179. Total +264 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, with medium confidence: current main rotates or surfaces overloaded failures after no auth-profile/fallback path remains, and the linked issue provides MiniMax overloaded logs. I did not run a live MiniMax outage because that failure is nondeterministic and this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is needed; the remaining decision is whether maintainers accept the default overloaded retry behavior and its compatibility/auth-provider/availability tradeoffs.

target.title: fix(agents): retry transient provider errors when single profile has no fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Rebase the existing overloaded-failover fix onto the current embedded-agent runner paths.
- Bound no-fallback same-profile overload handling to one retry with backoff for both prompt-side and assistant-side failures.
- Keep non-overload failures on the existing surface/fallback paths and add exhaustion coverage for the bounded retry behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Issue #84236 reports MiniMax returning `overloaded_error` / `server is busy, please retry later`, after which the agent stopped without the expected retry/failover behavior. This patch makes the no-fallback single-profile overloaded path retry the same profile once with backoff, then surface a 503 overloaded error if the next attempt is still overloaded.
- **Real environment tested:** Current PR worktree `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-84265` at `06f14170da79060b369af69fbf9b9caeda6c1a20`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `023a101b91382fef21b76d1e5a70e0aff581a3ce`. Runtime startup used isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr-84265-live-19166` on dedicated port `19166`, channels skipped, and the gateway reached ready from the current PR code. The issue-specific MiniMax overloaded decision path was exercised through the embedded runner failover function with the same provider/model/error shape from the issue.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `DAILY_FIX_CONTEXT=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/context.env /media/vdc/code/ai/Hermes/.claude/skills/daily-fix/scripts/daily_fix.sh check-main-drift`; `OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-84265 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19166 OPENCLAW_LOG=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/dev-gateway-19166.log /media/vdc/code/ai/Hermes/.claude/skills/daily-fix/scripts/start_openclaw_dev_gateway.sh`; `TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-84265 node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/same-profile-overload-proof.mjs`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.run-embedded-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.
- **Evidence after fix:** `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/verification-summary-current.md`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/check-main-drift.json`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/dev-gateway-19166.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/same-profile-overload-proof-current.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/verification-marker.json`.
- **Observed result after fix:** The PR is current with `origin/main` and no longer has merge drift. The dev gateway reached `ready` from the rebased PR. The proof script shows the first overloaded MiniMax result returns `{ "action": "retry", "retryKind": "same_profile_transient", "sameProfileOverloadRetries": 1 }`, records `same-profile-backoff` with `delayMs: 1000`, and the second overloaded result returns `{ "action": "throw", "reason": "overloaded", "status": 503, "provider": "minimax", "model": "MiniMax-M2.7" }`. Targeted Vitest passed 3 shards: `33 + 18 + 30` tests.
- **What was not tested:** A live MiniMax outage was not forced against the external provider because provider overload is nondeterministic and not controllable from the test environment; the external Telegram delivery path was not exercised.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.run-embedded-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts`

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.run-embedded-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The no-fallback overloaded path reused the generic run-loop budget after same-profile retry was introduced, so persistent overloaded responses could keep retrying the same provider before surfacing. The retry cap is scoped to overloaded failures; rate-limit, auth, billing, and other provider failures keep their existing surface or fallback behavior.
